subject: Office Practice
q:
Read the case below carefully and answer this question

PROBLEMS CAUSED BY THE MANAGING DIRECTOR OF OKF LIMITED
OKF limited, an initially fast growing company, was established twelve years ago to deal in office machines. The vision of the Managing Director, Dr. Rock Bansco was that the Company would become the leading supplier of office machines. He also hoped that the company would provide employment for many secondary school leavers.
Within the first five years of the company's inception, its profit grew rapidly. The customers increased so much that by the sixth year of the company's operation, the staff strength had become more than one hundred. However, all the workers who were employed in its sixth year of operation were both unqualified and inexperienced, worse still, they were given supervisory positions because of their relationship to the Managing Director in one way or the other.

As a large company, OKF Limited had six Heads of Department. However, the Managing Director hardly involved any of them in the day-to-day running of the company. He did all recruitment, purchases as well as salaries and wages administration. Staff salaries and wages were also very poor. As time went on, virtually all the staff became unhappy and this affected their morale.

The Managing Director recently called an emergency meeting to address the staff, surprisingly, he did not appoint anyone to record the proceedings of the meeting.
 

a) State two problems in OKF Limited.

b) Outline departments in OKF Limited that should be responsible for the following activities:

  • recruitment of workers
  • sales promotion
  • acquisition of machines
  • payments

c) i) When is an emergency meeting called by the management of an organisation?

ii) What is the meeting's related title of the officer who should have recorded the meeting proceedings?

iii) State two duties the officer in (c)(ii) should have carried out before the day of the meeting

iv) List five headings under which the officer would have recorded the proceedings of the meeting.
explanation:
a) - Inexperience workers or staff.
- Lack of delegation of duties.

b) - Recruitment of workers is done by personnel department.
- Sales promotion is carried out by sales and marketing department
- Acquisition of machines: Purchasing department
- Payments: Finance department/Account department.

c) i) When there is urgent need to discuss and act prompt on some issues affecting the organisation.
ii) The Secretary is the officer who should have recorded the meeting proceedings.
iii) - He prepared the agenda with the help of the chairman.
- He prepares and sends the notice of meeting to members.
- He circulates documents needed for the meeting.
- He receives apologies from members who are not able to attend the meeting.
- He prepares the chairman's agenda.

iv) - Heading
- Participants
- Approval of previous minutes
- Action items
- Announcement
- Next meeting
- Adjournment
- Signature line

subject: Office Practice
q:
a) i) Explain this office document: Delivery note.
ii) Explain this office document: Receipt.
iii) Explain this office document: Bin card.
iv) Explain this office document: Quotation.

 

b) Identify the types of equipment which would be used to perform this task in an office;

i) attaching a cheque to a letter to be sent to suppliers
ii) boring holes on letters received in the registry for filing.
iii) getting a carbon copy of a query letter to a worker.
iv) sending an exact copy of a document to an overseas partner.
v) making more copies of minutes of a meeting.
vi) disposing of unwanted documents.

 

c) Explain this type of mail:

i) Personal mail
ii) Confidential mail
iii) Urgent mail
explanation:
a) i)Delivery note: This is a document sent by the seller to the buyer for signature when goods are delivered to him and it will serve as evidence that delivery has been made. It is used when goods are transported by the seller's means of transportation
ii) Receipt: This is an acknowledgment of money received from the buyer by the seller. It is a document which confirms that payment has been made. It is prepared by the seller and sent to the buyer stating the total amount received. It is an evidence of payment.
iii) Bin Card: This shows the record of receipts and issues of materials in the store. It is kept by the store keeper to record entries balance of stock at a particular time.
iv) Quotation: This is the supplier's reply to the letter of enquiry which states the price, terms of payment, delivery dates and any discount. It is a statement of price given to a buyer when he asks his supplier for quotation.

 

b) i) Stapling machine
ii) Perforating machine
iii) Carbon paper
iv) Fax machine
v) Photocopy machine
vi) Shredding machine

 

c) i) Personal mails: These are mails addressed to particular individual. They are normally opened by the person to whom they are addressed.
ii) Confidential mails: These are mails shared with only a few people for furthering certain purposes, it is a mail that people should not have access to . It is always sealed and stamped.
iii) Urgent mail: This type of mail require prompt attention. Once a mail is marked 'urgent', it should be attended to first before others. The contents should be checked immediately to facilitate speedy action.

q:
THE MEETING RELATED PROBLEM AT THE STAFF CO-OPERATION SOCIETY OF JEFFERSON PLC

Dr Andrew iheanacho and Mrs. Adamu Suliyat were elected as Chairman and Secretary respectively of the staff Co-operative Society of Jefferson Plc in December 28,2017, prior to the election,the offices of the chairman and General Secretary were held by Mrs.Idowu Ibrahim and Miss Alamu Rexson respectively.

During the regime of Mrs.Idowu Ibrahim and Miss Alamu Rexson members used to complain that meetings were not properly convened.Some of the meetings were postposed or adjourned.The Chairman's incompetence because obvious to most members of the Society and Staff of the Company alike. For instance, meeting of the society were always very rowdy as the Chairman was in the habit of losing control over members.

At a particular meeting held on 15th of February 2017, a motion was put to vote,Mallam Abubakar, who was elected to be in charge of voting during meetings was absent ,Consequently, Dr Aba was mandated by the Chairman to act on behalf. Shortly thereafter, a member drew the Chairman's attention to the fact that more than half of the sixty members who attended the meeting had already left while the meeting was still in progress. This observation was confirmed by another member who stressed that most of the members that left did so without taking permission from the Chairman.

a) Identify two other duties of Mallam Abubakar at the Staff Co-operative Society meetings.

b) State three rules the new secretary should observe to ensure that the staff Co-operative Society meetings are properly convened.

c) State five roles of Dr. Iheanacho to ensure that meetings of the Staff Co-operative Society are properly conducted.
explanation:
a) - Mallam Abubakar is the one incharge of giving permission to those that wants to leave the meeting.

- Mallam Abubakar is also the one incharge of voting during meetings.

 

b) - No member is allowed to talk or leave the meeting without the permission of the Chairman.

- All members should be present at the meeting when it will be held except on on official errand for the society.

- All duties and rules of the meeting should be adhere to and followed.

 

c) - He must be present at the meeting at all time.

- He must ensure that all rules are followed by all the members.

- He must ensure that all members are controlled during the meeting.

- He must ensure that the members vote during the voting period

- He must ensure that the meeting are convened properly and well structured for better understanding to the members

subject: Office Practice
q:
a) i) What is a report?
ii) List and explain two channels of communications.

b) List and explain four parts of an investigation report
explanation:
a) i)  A report is a document that presents information in an organized format for a specific audience and purpose.It is also a well researched,planned and organized documents that are written for a purpose. Summaries of a reports may be delivered orally,complete reports are almost always in the form of written documents.

ii) - Face to face communication
- Written methods of communication

- Face to face communication: One of the richest channels of communication that can be used within an organization.physical presence,the tone of the speaker's voice and facial expressions help recipients of a message interpret that message as the speaker intends.

- Written methods of communication: It is used when a message that does not require interaction needs to be communicated to an employee or group. Policies, letters, memos, manuals, notice ans announcements are all messages that work well for this channel

 

b) - Referral source
- Allegation details
- Information about the subject
- Information to identify the case

- Information to identify the case: Being the report with case specific information that identifies the case the report is related to.Include such as the investigator's name,case number,the date the case was entered and the date it was assigned to the investigator.
- Referral source: The next section should include the compliant's information.The compliant's work phone number,e-mail,employee number,office location,department and job title help identify the person lodging the compliant
- Allegation details: Harassment,discrimination,retaliation- what type of allegation is under investigation? In this section, be sure to include as much details as possible about the initial compliant
- Information about the subject: The information required for this section of the report is similar to that of the Referral source" section.However,it's the subject's information being documented this time.

q:
a) State four qualities of a good filling system.

b) Janet has an interview to attend next Monday for the position of a receiptionist. List and explain four qualities the interviewers would expect from the applicant.
explanation:
a) - The compact filing system should be adopted by every business office. It means that the filing system should not require any unnecessary space.
- The filing system should be simple and not too elaborate.At the same time, the usefulness of the filing system cannot be sacrificed for the sack of simplicity
- A good filing system should be arranged in such a way that the records are easily available whenever required. The filing system should allow the insertion of additional documents without disturbing the existing order of files
- The filing system should be economical in time,space,money and operations.The cost of installation and operation of filing system should be as low as possible.
- The filling system can be expanded if the volume of business transactions increased. An inflexible system is not useful after crossing a certain limit.

 

b) - Effective communication
-Professionalism
- Interpersonal aplomb
- Multitasking

- Effective communication: Naturally, a receptionist should have excellent verbal communication skills. Active listening and great customer service skills also are a must.

- Professionalism: As the first person your customers,visitors and future employees encounter,the receptionist needs to make a good first impression.Professional appearance and attitude is what to be considered.

- Interpersonal aplomb: Good interpersonal skills go beyond basic communication abilities.Soft skills like friendliness and likability are especially important for the receptionist role.

- Multitasking capabilities: Receptionist have multiple responsibilities, which they must often juggle simultaneously.These could include handling incoming call traffic while running a busy reception area.

b) Explain the following types of meeting
i) Emergency meeting
ii) Committee meeting
iii) Board meeting

c) List and explain three types of indexing
explanation:
a) - Sender's address
- Date
- Recipients' address
- Salutation

b) i) Emergency meeting is a meeting specially called to discuss a particular item of a company's business,usually one of some importance.The meeting may be called by a group of shareholders or by the directors.
ii) Committee meeting consists of a named subgroup of people within an organization who come together to fill a predetermined function. A committee's work is described in its charter and is often conducted in a series of meetings.

subject: Office Practice
q:
a) What is office organization?

b) Explain the following types of mail;
i) Urgent mail
ii) Personal mail
iii) Confidential mail
iv) Official mail

c) List five equipment in an organization's mail room and state one use of each
explanation:
a) Office organization is the process of defining, grouping office activities and establishing authority relationship among the employees who are working in an office so that they can be executed assigned activities effectively and economically

b) i) Urgent mail is compelling immediate action or attention,pressing,Conveying a sense of pressing importance i.e an urgent message.
ii) Personal mail is an email message from an individual to another on a subject that is informal and private.This kind of mail is common between friends, relations, and acquaintances.Personal mail is also an email sent by an employee within an employer's server but which serve no business purpose.
iii) Confidential mail is a mail that is protected i.e to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access. Confidential mode is set to set an expiration date for messages or revoke access at any time.
iv) Official mail is mail sent from, or by, an authorized department of government,government agency or international organization and normally has some indication that it is official; a certifying catchet,return address or other means of identity,indicating its user.

 

c) - Envelope Sealers
- Wafer Sealers/ Tabbers
- Feeders
- Folders and inserters
- Label appliers and stamp affixes

- Envelope Sealers: Envelope sealers automate the sealing process.Pieces are transported through where the gum flaps is moistened and the flap pressed closed.There are manual as well as automated envelop sealers varying by speed,piece size and bin capacity etc.

- Wafer Sealers/ Tabbers : Wafer Sealers and Mail Tabbing equipment save time and money in the mailroom. These machines automatically apply mail tabs/wafer seals to a wide range of media including mail piece,folded stock,double postcards,newsletters, and booklets etc

- Feeders: Easy to use and designed to maximize printing, Tabbing/Labelling and stamp affixing operations.Designed for in-line operations and ease of use,once set-up they require very little user interaction aside from keeping the material bin full.

- Folders and inserters: A folder inserter folds a sheet of paper,inserts it into an envelope, and then seals the envelope.

- Label appliers and stamp affixers: Help automate the process of addressing and personalizing mail piece saving time and money in the mailroom.

q:
OVERCOMING THE CHALLENGES AT THEOLAS INTERNATIONAL COMPANY

Prior to Mr.Okonkwo's employment as the Human Resource Manager of Theolas International Company, the organization was faced with the problem of absenteeism by its workers. They came late to work and left before the closing time. As a result of this, the volume of production of the company reduced. This inturn, reduced the income of the organization so much that payments of workers' wages and salaries became difficult. Many experienced workers began to leave the firm for greener pastures.

The firm also found it difficult to meet its financial obligations to suppliers. On assumption of duty, Mr. Okonkwo quickly swung into action. He carried out an investigation to ascertain the causes of these problems. One of his findings was that workers bitterly complained about their conditions of service. Although their wages and salaries were acceptable, they asked for other benefits from the company which were denied them

Mr. Okonkwo also discovered that workers were employed without following due process. Finally, he found out that no measure was put in place to regulate the punctuality of workers. Armed with these finding, Mr Okonkwo addressed each of the issues and put in place adequate measures to ensure proper supervision and satisfaction of workers.


(a). Suggest one measure that Mr. Okonkwo could use to improve staff regularity at work

(b).  Apart from irregularity at work, state two other problems in Theolas International Company

(c). List six welfare packages that would improve workers satisfaction at Theolas International Company

(d). State four pre-employment activities the company should have carried out in recruiting its staff
explanation:
(a). Mr Okonkwo should put up a measure that would regulate the punctuality of workers after the company fulfills its own obligation.

 

(b). - The volume of production of the company reduced which led to the reduction of the organisation's income which as well made it difficult to pay worker's salaries and wages.

- The firm also found it difficult to meet its financial obligations to suppliers.

 

(c). 

- Reward the workers
- Recognize the workers
- Training and investment
- Compensation
- Drive communication and transparency
- Promote good health
- Allow employees to shape their own roles
- Correct planning of employee's schedules

 

(d). - identify the hring need
- Devise a recruitment plan
- Write a job description
- Adertise the position
- Recruit the position
- Review applications
- Screening
- Interviews

q:
(a). Outline three principles of a good organization

(b). State three advantages derived from office correspondence

(c). Explan the following methods of payment
i. Money order
ii. Postal order
iii. Cheque
iv. Standing order
explanation:
(a). 

- The principle of authority: Responsibility for results should correspond with manager's authority and influence.

- The principle of span of control: Span of control refers to span of supervision or management or authority and responsibility. It stands for the number of subordinates seeking the direction, guidance and supervision from their immediate superior.

- The principle of balance: Balance in organisation structure does not stand for equal emphasis on size and number of people working; it does not mean equality per se. There should be balance from the point view of overall effectiveness.

 

(b). 

- The basic purpose of any business letter is to convey information regarding business activities. Information can be transmitted through business letter to customers, suppliers, debtors, government authorities, financial institutions, bank and insurance companies and to any other parties related with the business.

- Memo: It can help maintain the good relationship among boss and subordinates, because the bossing attitude is absent here

- The main objective of memo is to inform the decisions and actions. For this purpose, it shouls be wriiten by higher authority.

 

(c)i. An order for the payment of a specified amount of money, usually issued and payable at a bank or post office.. It remain a trusted financial instrument.

ii. A postal order or postal note is a type of money order usually intended for sending money through the mail. It is purchased at a post office and is payable at another post office to the named recipient.

iii. A cheque is a written order to a bank to pay a stated sum from the drawer's account, written on a specially printed form. It is also a document that orders a bank to pay a specific amount of money from a person's account to the person in whose name the cheque has been issued.

iv. A standing order is an instruction a bank account holder gives to their bank to pay a set amount at regular intervals to another's accoun. The instruction is sometimes known as a banker's order.

q:
(a). As a receptionist of a Manufacturing company, outline any five actions you would take to handle a telephone call from a customer

(b). The clerk at Toppy Tee has been directed to file a new customer's letter. Meanwhile, there is no file in the cabinet that is relevant to the subject matter of the letter.
  Outline five actions the office clerk would take in filling the letter.
explanation:
(a). - Be polite: Have a standard greeting and use it each time you pick up the phone.

- Stay organized: Take detailed notes. Pay attention to what time calls came in and write down caller ID. Have the company directory at your fingertips for last transfer.

- Listen carefully; Practice active lsitening. Focus on what the caller is saying and quickly determine where to transfer them to best serve their needs.

- Communicate effectively: Don't mumble, speak too quickly, or sound forlorn. Do not enunciate properly, speak at a conformtable pace,and assist callers with energy and enthusiasm.

- Verify: Take a moment to repeat the caller's number back to him and make sure it's correct. If you are unsure how to spell a caller's name, ask her. confirm the most important details of the call before passing the message along to its intended recipient

- Ask the caller's permission: Before placing someone on hold, ask the caller if it's ok.

- Say thank you: If the caller says they will hold, say than you.

 

(b) - Receiving the document; If it is a letter or document that came through the mail, record it in the mail received register and write the date received or date stamp it.

- Action: Forward the letter to the person that has to deal with it.

- Follow up: Check that the letter has been dealt with

- Collecting the lettter; All letters and two copies of the replies must be collected in a filing tray

- Filing: Choose a regular time to file

explanation: In a line structure organization, a superior exercises direct supervision over a subordinate. subject: Office Practice
q: The secreatary at ABC Company needs to produce a document in large quantity. Which of these office equipment would be used?
explanation:
A duplicator is used to produce a large number of copies of a document. It works by creating a master copy of the document and then using it to
print the copies. This is different from a typewriter, which is used for typing individual documents, or a tele-printer, which is used for sending and
receiving typed messages. A Risograph is a high-speed digital printing system designed for high-volume copying and printing, but it's not as
commonly used as a duplicator in an office setting.

subject: Office Practice
q: A few copies of a written message to all members of staff in a large organization could be posted on
explanation:
Notice boards are typically used in large organizations to post written messages for all staff members to see. They are usually placed in common
areas where they are easily visible. Billboards are large outdoor boards for displaying advertisements, and magic boards are interactive
whiteboards used for presentations. Signboards are used to display information or directions, but they are not typically used to post messages for
staff members.

subject: Office Practice
q: A member who raises a motion at a meeting is a
explanation:
In the context of a meeting, the person who raises a motion is known as the proposer. The proposer initiates a process of debate and decision-
making on a particular issue. A scrutinizer is someone who examines something closely, a convener is a person who brings together a group of
people for a meeting, and a proxy is a person authorized to act for another.

subject: Office Practice
q: Miss Rosey, the clerical assistant at XYZ Ltd, keeps documents in the file according to dates received. This system of filing is
explanation:
Chronological filing is a method of filing documents in order of their date, either ascending or descending. This system is often used for things like
invoices, bank statements, or reports that are often referred to by the date they were created or processed. In the given scenario, Miss Rosey is
keeping documents in the file according to dates received, which is a characteristic of chronological filing.

subject: Office Practice
q: Ojo was aksed by his superior in the office to check up the continent where Australia is located. He would get his information from
explanation:
An atlas is a collection of maps; it is typically a map of Earth or a region of Earth, but there are atlases of the other planets (and their satellites) in
the Solar System. Atlases have traditionally been bound into book form, but today many atlases are in multimedia formats. In the given scenario,
Ojo can find the information about the continent where Australia is located in an atlas.

subject: Office Practice
q: The minutes of APX ventures meeting written by secretary was considered to be alright by members. This implies the the minutes is
explanation:
The minutes of a meeting are the official record of what was discussed and decided at that meeting. They should be accurate and include all the
important details. If the minutes of APX ventures meeting written by the secretary were considered to be alright by members, this implies that the
minutes were accurate.

subject: Office Practice
q: If goods are returned, the seller issues
explanation:
When goods are returned by the buyer, the seller issues a credit note. A credit note is a document sent by a seller to its buyer, or, in other words, a
vendor to the customer, notifying that a credit has been provided to their account. The reason for issuing a credit note are typically due to some
returned goods or an overcharge on an invoice.

subject: Office Practice
q: Sule, the mail clerk at Ali Ltd, files some documents under two headings. For easy location of such document, he would make use of
explanation:
Cross-referencing is the method Sule would use for easy location of such documents. Cross-referencing is a process of referencing an index or a
point of data in a document or book to another piece of data elsewhere in the same document or book. It helps in locating information quickly and easily.

subject: Office Practice
q: One of the advantages of visual communication is that it
options: can be kept for future use
does not require extra skill to interpret
explanation:
One of the advantages of visual communication is that it can be kept for future use. Visual communication involves the use of visual elements, such
as drawings, illustrations and electronic images, to convey ideas and information. These visual elements can be stored and retrieved for future use.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of the following reference books contains information about parliamentary proceedings?
explanation:
Hansards are the traditional name of the transcripts of Parliamentary Debates in Britain and many Commonwealth countries. It is named after
Thomas Curson Hansard, a London printer and publisher, who was the first official printer to the parliament. Therefore, Hansards contain
information about parliamentary proceedings.

subject: Office Practice
q: One of the essential parts of a report is
explanation:
A report is a document that presents information in an organized format for a specific audience and purpose. One of the essential parts of a report is
the conclusion. The conclusion summarizes the report, it includes the results, analysis, and the final thoughts. It is where the author brings together
their findings and comments on them.

subject: Office Practice
q: Oral communication could be done through
explanation:
Oral communication refers to the spoken word in the communication process. Oral communication can be either face-to-face communication or a
conversation over the phone or on the voice chat over the internet. Therefore, telephones are a means through which oral communication could be done.

subject: Office Practice
q: One of the advantages of lateral filing system is that it
options: reduces the probability of losing documents
explanation:
A lateral filing system is a method of storing files horizontally in a cabinet. The advantage of this system is that it economizes the use of space. This
is because files are stored side by side, which allows for more files to be stored in the same amount of space compared to vertical filing systems.

subject: Office Practice
q: A correspondence could be described as
options: a formal speech presented at an occasion
a message conveyed through the grapevine
a written communication between two organizations
explanation:
Correspondence refers to the exchange of written communication between two parties. This can be between individuals, organizations, or both.
Therefore, a correspondence could be described as a written communication between two organizations.

subject: Office Practice
q:
A chief accountant tasked a five-member committee to look into an embezzlement case. After the completion of the assignment, the committee is
required to come up with a
explanation:
In a business setting, when a committee is tasked to look into a case, they are usually required to come up with a report after the completion of the
assignment. This report would detail their findings and possibly recommendations. Therefore, the committee is required to come up with a report.

subject: Office Practice
q: The acronym NIPOST stands for
explanation: The acronym NIPOST stands for Nigerian Postal Service. It is the company responsible for providing postal services in Nigeria.

subject: Office Practice
q: One of the disadvantages of oral communication is that
explanation:
Oral communication is a type of communication where we use words to exchange information with others. This can be face-to-face, over the phone,
on the radio or television or other media. One of the disadvantages of oral communication is that it can be easily denied as there is often no record
of the conversation. This makes it difficult to prove what was said or agreed upon.

subject: Office Practice
q: The procedure to be followed by a mailroom clerk in handling incoming mails is
explanation:
The correct procedure for handling incoming mails by a mailroom clerk is: receiving, sorting, opening, and scrutinizing. Receiving is the first step
where the clerk accepts the mail. Sorting is the next step where the mail is categorized based on certain criteria. Opening is the third step where the
mail is opened to reveal its contents. Scrutinizing is the final step where the contents of the mail are carefully examined.

subject: Office Practice
q:
The Board of Directors of ABC & Co has just decided to reduce the workforce. The letter conveying this policy to the Managing Director for
implementation would be marked
explanation:
When the Board of Directors of a company decides to reduce the workforce, the letter conveying this policy to the Managing Director for
implementation would be marked as 'confidential'. This is because such information is sensitive and should only be known to those who need to
know. Confidentiality is a set of rules or a promise that restricts access to or dissemination of information.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of the following means of written communications would an accountant use to convey a short directive to an accounts clerk?
explanation:
A memorandum, often shortened to memo, is a type of document used for internal communication between colleagues or members of a department
within a company. It is used to convey information, and often directives, to employees. Therefore, an accountant would use a memorandum to
convey a short directive to an accounts clerk.

subject: Office Practice
q: When a meeting decides that a matter should lie on the table, it means that the matter should
options: be discussed at the next meeting
be discussed at the executive level
explanation:
In the context of a meeting, when it is decided that a matter should 'lie on the table', it means that the matter is being temporarily set aside. The
matter is not being dismissed entirely, but rather postponed for discussion at a later time, typically at the next meeting.

subject: Office Practice
q: The section of an office in Rammy Ltd where mails are handled is
explanation:
The registry is the section of an office where mails are handled. It is responsible for the receipt, recording, distribution, storage, and disposal of mail
in an organization. Therefore, in Rammy Ltd, mails would be handled in the registry.

subject: Office Practice
q: Port Avenue & Co deals with lots of client. For the company receptionist to relate effectively with them, she would need to possess good
explanation:
A receptionist is the first point of contact for a company's clients. Therefore, they need to possess good communication skills to effectively interact
with clients, understand their needs, and relay information accurately. While typing, filing, and marketing skills may be beneficial, communication
is the most critical skill for a receptionist.

subject: Office Practice
q: The principle Unity of Command in an office implies that
options: no worker in an organization should report to more than a two-line supervisor
every worker in an organization should be directly responsible to the general manager
everyone in the organization should be able to work in all departments
no worker in an organization should report to more than one-line supervisior
explanation:
The principle of Unity of Command states that an employee should receive orders from one superior only. This prevents confusion and conflicts
about how work should be done. It ensures clear lines of communication and responsibility. Therefore, the correct answer is that no worker in an
organization should report to more than one-line supervisor.

subject: Office Practice
q: What does it mean when a meeting is adjourned sine die?
options: The meeting was fixed for another date
No date was fixed for its continuation
explanation:
When a meeting is adjourned sine die, it means that it has been postponed indefinitely. The Latin term 'sine die' translates to 'without a day',
meaning no date has been set for its continuation. Therefore, the correct answer is that no date was fixed for its continuation.

subject: Office Practice
q: At Adesco Plc, Mr Jack is the one who assigns duties to all the office workers. Mr.Jack is acting as the
explanation:
In a company, the person who assigns duties to all the office workers is typically the Manager. The manager is responsible for planning, directing,
and overseeing the operations and fiscal health of a business unit, division, department, or an operating unit within an organization. The manager is
also responsible for delegating and directing tasks and operations to employees, managing performance and project goals, providing constructive
feedback, and resolving team issues.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of these items could be found in the reception?
explanation:
In the reception area of an office, you are likely to find items like newspapers and magazines. These are usually provided for visitors to read while
they wait for their appointments. The other options listed such as inward and outward registers, attendance registers, and dispatch books are
typically not found in the reception area, but rather in other parts of the office.

subject: Office Practice
q: The purpose of a notice of meeting is to inform members of the
options: time, date and venue of a proposed meeting
decision taken at the previous meeting
items to be discussed at the meeting
explanation:
The purpose of a notice of meeting is to inform members of the time, date, and venue of a proposed meeting. This allows members to plan their
schedules accordingly and ensures that they are aware of when and where the meeting will take place. The other options listed such as decisions
taken at the previous meeting, postponement of a proposed meeting, and items to be discussed at the meeting are typically included in the agenda
or minutes of the meeting, not the notice.

subject: Office Practice
q: The principles of office organization which requires workers to be assigned duties according to their skills and qualifications is
explanation:
The principle of office organization that requires workers to be assigned duties according to their skills and qualifications is 'specialization'.
Specialization refers to the concept of focusing on a specific area or task, allowing individuals to hone their skills in that particular area, leading to
increased efficiency and productivity.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of the following activties does the handling of incoming mails involve?
explanation:
The handling of incoming mails involves 'Examining the content'. This is a crucial step in the process of handling incoming mail, as it allows the
recipient to understand the purpose of the mail and take appropriate action. The other options listed are not typically involved in the handling of
incoming mail.

subject: Office Practice
q: In a closed office layout, workers enjoy
explanation:
In a closed office layout, workers enjoy 'relative comfort'. A closed office layout refers to a workspace design where each employee has their own
individual office space. This can provide a more comfortable work environment as it offers more privacy and less noise compared to an open office
layout. The other options listed may not necessarily be true in a closed office layout.

subject: Office Practice
q: A letter from Mr Jack to convince XYZ company to employ Miss Zizi is
explanation:
A letter from Mr Jack to convince XYZ company to employ Miss Zizi is a recommendation letter. A recommendation letter is a document in which
the writer assesses the qualities, characteristics, and capabilities of the person being recommended in terms of that individual's ability to perform a
particular task or function. In this case, Mr Jack is recommending Miss Zizi for employment.

subject: Office Practice
q: The department in an organization that monitors the implementation of a company's policies is
explanation:
The administrative department in an organization is responsible for monitoring the implementation of a company's policies. The administrative
department is responsible for the day-to-day operations of the company, including enforcing company policies and procedures.

subject: Office Practice
q: A receptionist is an employee who
options: supervises the junior employees in the office
directs visitors to the office they wants to see
prepares itineraries for his superior officers
operates varieties of equipment in the office
explanation:
A receptionist is an employee who directs visitors to the office they want to see. The receptionist is often the first point of contact for clients and
visitors, and their role includes directing visitors to the appropriate office or person.

subject: Office Practice
q: Communication to a large number of people in different locations simultaneously could be done by means of
explanation:
Communication to a large number of people in different locations simultaneously is best done through advertisements. Advertisements can be
broadcasted or published across various platforms (TV, radio, internet, print media etc.) reaching a large audience at the same time. The other
options like telephone, merchandizing, and display are not as effective for simultaneous communication to a large audience in different locations.

subject: Office Practice
q: During a recent workshop, participants were advised to always provide good information. In order words, any information given by them should be
explanation:
Good information should be timely. This means it should be provided at the right time or when it is needed. Timely information helps in making
accurate and effective decisions. Futuristic, long, or short does not necessarily define good information. Information can be long or short, but if it's
not timely, it may not be useful.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of these factors would a purchasing officer consider in selecting an office equipment?
explanation:
In selecting an office equipment, a purchasing officer would consider its suitability. This means whether the equipment is appropriate or fit for the
intended purpose or use in the office. Control, location, and colour are not primary factors in selecting office equipment. The functionality and
suitability of the equipment for the office tasks are more important.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of the following activities is part of the procedure for handling outgoing mails?
explanation:
Stamping is part of the procedure for handling outgoing mails. It involves affixing postage stamps to the mail to indicate that the postage fee has
been paid. Coding, opening, and sorting are typically associated with incoming mail, not outgoing mail.

subject: Office Practice
q:
Bodex co.Ltd sends out lots of mails. Which of the following office machines would help to remove the burden of affixing postage stamps on each
envelope?
explanation:
A franking machine is used to print a postage mark directly onto envelopes or labels, which can then be affixed to mail. This removes the need to
manually affix postage stamps to each envelope, making it an ideal solution for a company like Bodex co.Ltd that sends out lots of mails. Collating
machines, addressing machines, and folding machines perform different functions and would not help with this task.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of these documents would be used to procure an item from the store?
explanation:
A requisition is a formal request to procure an item from the store. It is typically used in business settings to request that specific goods be ordered.
A quotation is a document that states a price for goods or services, an invoice is a bill for goods or services provided, and a receipt is a document
acknowledging that a person has received money or property in payment.

subject: Office Practice
q: A form of correspondence used in conveying information to members of the public is
explanation:
A press release is a form of correspondence used to convey information to members of the public. It is a written communication that reports
specific but brief information about an event, circumstance, or other happening. It's typically tied to a business or organization and is provided to
media through a variety of means.

subject: Office Practice
q: A group of people appointed by the parent committee to deal with a special matter is
explanation:
An ad hoc committee is a group of people appointed by the parent committee to deal with a special matter. The term 'ad hoc' is a Latin phrase that
literally means 'for this', implying created or done for a particular purpose as necessary. The committee is usually disbanded after the task is completed.

subject: Office Practice
q:
A secretary has found it difficult to type a manuscript given to her by the manager due to poor handwriting. In other words, the secretary has the
challenge of
options: poorly decoded manuscript by the manager
poor encoded manuscript by the manager
explanation:
The secretary is having difficulty typing the manuscript due to poor handwriting. This means the manuscript was poorly encoded by the manager.
Encoding in this context refers to the process of putting thoughts into symbols for communication, which in this case is the manager's handwriting.

subject: Office Practice
q:
At Zeinab & Sons Plc, envelopes are fastened with the use of gum and this takes so much time. The machine which would help overcome this
challenge is
explanation:
A sealing machine is used to seal envelopes, which would speed up the process at Zeinab & Sons Plc. A collating machine is used to arrange papers
in a specific order, a folding machine is used to fold papers, and a jogging machine is used to align stacks of paper - none of these would help with
sealing envelopes.

subject: Office Practice
q: A letter containing valuable documents would be sent by
explanation:
Registered mail is a mail service offered by postal services in many countries, which allows the sender proof of mailing via a mailing receipt and,
upon request, electronic verification that an article was delivered or that a delivery attempt was made. This makes it the most secure way to send a
letter containing valuable documents. Electronic mail wouldn't be suitable for physical documents, and surface handling and airmail don't provide
the same level of security.

subject: Office Practice
q: In communication process, writing a memo is an example of
explanation:
In the communication process, writing a memo is an example of encoding. Encoding is the process of turning thoughts into communication. The
encoder uses a 'medium' to send the message -- a phone call, email, text message, face-to-face meeting, or other communication tool. The level of
conscious thought that goes into encoding messages depends on the individual.

subject: Office Practice
q: Which of these organizational structures is also referred to as military form of organization?
explanation:
The Line organizational structure is also referred to as the military form of organization. This is because, in this structure, authority flows
vertically from top to bottom with clear lines of command and control, similar to the military.

subject: Office Practice
q: A document sent by the buyer to the seller when the goods supplied are not satisfactory is
explanation:
When the goods supplied are not satisfactory, the buyer sends a letter of complaints to the seller. This letter is used to express dissatisfaction for a
product or service and to request for a remedy, which could be a refund, replacement, or repair of the goods.
